title: Understand: thermal energy and equilibrium
description: Check your understanding of thermal energy and thermal equilibrium in this set of free practice questions.

assessment_items.question_content:
**Complete the statement.** 

> A system’s thermal energy refers to  \_\_\_\_\_\_.

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: the kinetic energy of its randomly moving particles
the heat that transfers from the system to the surroundings

assessment_items.question_content:
**Complete the statement.** 

> The thermal energy of a system is \_\_\_\_\_\_.

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: the kinetic energy of its randomly moving particles
the temperature at which it changes state
the heat that transfers from the system to the surroundings

assessment_items.question_content:
**Complete the statement.** 

> The thermal energy of a system refers to  \_\_\_\_\_\_.

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: the kinetic energy of its randomly moving particles
the heat that transfers from the system to the surroundings

assessment_items.question_content:
**Complete the statement.**

Heat is best described as [[☃ dropdown 1]] that spontaneously transfers from a [[☃ dropdown 2]] system to a [[☃ dropdown 3]] system.
hints: Heat is thermal energy that transfers from one system to another.
Heat always flows spontaneously from a warmer system to a cooler system.

So the complete statement is:

Heat is best described as thermal energy that spontaneously transfers from a warmer system to a cooler system.

assessment_items.question_content:
**Complete the statement.**

Thermal energy spontaneously transfers from a [[☃ dropdown 1]] system to a [[☃ dropdown 2]] system. The energy that transfers is called [[☃ dropdown 3]].
hints: Thermal energy always transfers spontaneously from a warmer system to a cooler system.
The thermal energy that transfers is called heat.

So the complete statement is:

Thermal energy spontaneously transfers from a warmer system to a cooler system. The energy that is transferred is called heat.

assessment_items.question_content:
**Complete the statement.**

Heat is [[☃ dropdown 3]] that transfers from a system with a [[☃ dropdown 1]] temperature to a system with a [[☃ dropdown 2]] temperature.
hints: Heat is thermal energy that transfers from one system to another.
Heat always flows spontaneously from a warmer system to a cooler system.

So the complete statement is:

Heat is thermal energy that transfers between a system with a higher temperature to a system with a lower temperature.

assessment_items.question_content:
In 1654, Ferdinando II de’ Medici created the first sealed-bulb thermometer to measure temperature. Bulb thermometers rely on the consistent expansion and contraction of liquid trapped inside the device.

[[☃ image 1]]

**Complete the statement.**

>Temperature is a measure of the \_\_\_\_\_\_.

[[☃ radio 1]]

[[☃ explanation 1]]

options.alt: A museum photograph showing a rack of de-medici thermometers. Each glass tube ends in a sealed glass ball.
options.caption: *A rack of de’ Medici thermometers*

choices.content: average kinetic energy of the particles of a system
total thermal energy contained in a system
heat entering or leaving a system

assessment_items.question_content:
A Galileo thermometer uses labeled bulbs filled with liquids of different densities to measure the approximate temperature. Depending on the temperature of the water, some bulbs float and others sink. The lowest floating bulb is closest to the actual water temperature.

[[☃ image 1]]

**Complete the statement.**

>Temperature measures the \_\_\_\_\_\_ of a substance.

[[☃ radio 1]]

options.alt: A photograph of a Galileo thermometer showing a few bulbs, each filled with a different color liquid and carrying a metal tag labeled with a specific temperature. These bulbs are floating in a glass tube filled with a clear liquid.
options.caption: *A Galileo thermometer is not very accurate, but it sure is colorful!*

choices.content: average kinetic energy of the particles

assessment_items.question_content:
A cooking thermometer uses a bimetallic strip sensor composed of two different metals bonded together. These metals expand at different rates when heated. This causes the strip to coil or uncoil, moving a needle along a temperature dial. 

[[☃ image 1]]

**Complete the statement.**

>Temperature measures the \_\_\_\_\_\_.

[[☃ radio 1]]

options.alt: A photograph of cooking thermometer stuck into a cooked piece of chicken.
options.caption: *A cooking thermometer in action*

choices.content: average kinetic energy of the particles of a system
heat entering or leaving a system
ability to transfer thermal energy through a system

assessment_items.question_content:
**Complete the statement.**

>Two touching objects are in thermal equilibrium when \_\_\_\_\_\_.

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: energy no longer transfers between them
they have changed temperature by the same amount

assessment_items.question_content:
Two substances are in contact. They both have the same temperature. 

**What is the best description of the substances?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: They are no longer exchanging energy.

assessment_items.question_content:
**Complete the statement.**

If two systems that are in contact are in thermal equilibrium, they must have the same [[☃ dropdown 1]].
hints: Two touching systems are in thermal equilibrium when their particles have the same average kinetic energy.
Temperature is the measure of the average kinetic energy of the particles in a system.

So, the correct answer is:

If two systems that are in contact are in thermal equilibrium, they must have the same temperature.
